title: Component Entities and Paths
breadcrumbs: Design Considerations for Performance and Scalability
text: Component Entities and Paths The modeled objects and the component data structure provide basic information about the component entities and access paths. This is information that you need to make correct estimates of the volumes and changing rates of the data in the information system. For access objects, you should have the following information: Name of the access object Primary key Candidate keys Indexes Attributes Volumes, such as the initial number of rows and the monthly growth rate Access frequency, for example, once a minute, or five times a day For each access path, you need the following information: Name of the access path Connected entities Related Uniface component Multiplicity of the relationship used Estimated access volumes Estimated access frequency Was this topic helpful?
